# Compression Streams support on all browsers, Android’s New Credential Manager, and more dev news!

<SocialEmbed platform="youtube" id="RiVJzlTdD68" />

## Description

TL;DR 366  | The Google Developer News Show

0:08 -  Compression Streams support on all browsers → https://goo.gle/3QOMNLd 
0:27 - Android with Credential Manager and passkeys → https://goo.gle/3Mu3hWv 
0:57 - More ways for users to identify independently security tested apps on Google Play → https://goo.gle/4632HWD 
1:25  - Developer solutions: Unlock and simplify your workflow → https://goo.gle/47ppAVp 

Here to bring you the latest developer news from across Google is Rody Davis. Tune in every week for a new episode, and let us know what you think of the latest announcements in the comments below.

Follow Google for Developers on Instagram → https://goo.gle/googledevs

Watch more #DevShow → https://goo.gle/GDevShow
Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers

#Google #Developers

## Transcript

**0:00** · \[MUSIC PLAYING\] RODY DAVIS: Hi.

**0:03** · I'm Rody for "The Developer Show," and this is your weekly update on the coolest developer news from Google.

### Compression Streams support on all browsers

**0:08** · First, we can head over to the web to talk about the new Compression Streams API.

**0:11** · And it's available in all three browsers.

**0:14** · This API makes it possible to compress and decompress using gzip or deflate formats and no longer requires you to include a compression library in your applications.

**0:23** · You can learn more about the API on web.dev.

**0:26** · Next, we can head over to Android to talk about the announcement of the public release of Credential Manager.

### Android with Credential Manager and passkeys

**0:32** · This also includes support for passkeys, which enable a seamless login experience without requiring passwords.

**0:38** · The new Credential Manager makes it easy to show multiple logins available for each website or app you're trying to log into.

**0:45** · And if you already have a preferred password manager, there's a new credential provider that allows you to use them with the new Credential Manager.

**0:52** · To learn more, check out Android developers.googleblog.com.

### More ways for users to identify independently security tested apps on Google Play

**0:57** · Next, when it comes to security in Android, privacy is extremely important.

**1:01** · And now Google Play will show a new banner if an app like a VPN has gone through security testing from an independent party.

**1:07** · These labels will help in building trust, influencing product decisions, and placing a critical role in consumer risk protection.

**1:15** · These banners are also to highlight developers that prioritize user safety and data transparency.

**1:20** · If you want to learn more, you can check out security.googleblog.com.

**1:24** · Finally, we have some exciting new launches on developers.google.com featuring three new solutions aimed at helping developers get started when building microservices, three-tier architectures, and serverless web apps.

### Developer solutions: Unlock and simplify your workflow

**1:35** · Each of these solutions contain a pathway, a video, one click to deploy on Google Cloud, and a quiz where you can earn a badge and save it to your developer profile.

**1:44** · You can learn more at developers.google.com/solutions.

**1:48** · To learn more about all this week's stories, make sure to check the description box below for all the links.

**1:53** · Please remember to like, subscribe, share, and stay safe.

**1:56** · I'm Rody for "The Developer Show."

**1:58** · Thanks for watching, and we'll see you next week.

**2:00** · \[MUSIC PLAYING\]
